agilitycompetition

Class ElapsedTime

  • java.lang.Object
    • agilitycompetition.ElapsedTime
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    java.lang.Comparable<ElapsedTime>

    public class ElapsedTime
extends java.lang.Object
implements java.lang.Comparable<ElapsedTime>
    Course: CSCI 160
    File: ElapsedTime.java
    Uses: nothing
    Extends: nothing
    Implements: Comparable
    Author:
    aapplin

    • Constructor Summary

      Constructors 
      Constructor and Description
      ElapsedTime()
      Default Constructor - Calls the parameterized constructor.
      ElapsedTime(int hours, int minutes, int seconds, int milliseconds)
      Parameterized Constructor - each input value is validated in that an input over the maximum for any single item will increment the measure above it.

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Static Methods Instance Methods Concrete Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      int compareTo(ElapsedTime that)
      compareTo (abstract method of the comparable Interface) is implemented to impose a natural ordering on a group of objects.
      int getHours()
      Accessor for the property hours
      int getMilliseconds()
      Accessor for the property milliseconds
      int getMinutes()
      Accessor for the property minutes
      int getSeconds()
      Accessor for the property seconds
      java.lang.String getTimeInWords()
      Constructs a string of words representing the elapsed time.
      static void main(java.lang.String[] args) 
      java.lang.String toString()
      adds leading zeros where needed to print the time in the form: nn:nn:nn:nnn including leading zeros

      • Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

        clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, wait, wait, wait

    • Constructor Detail

      • ElapsedTime

        public ElapsedTime()
        Default Constructor - Calls the parameterized constructor.

      • ElapsedTime

        public ElapsedTime(int hours,
                   int minutes,
                   int seconds,
                   int milliseconds)
        Parameterized Constructor - each input value is validated in that an input over the maximum for any single item will increment the measure above it. So 1000 milliseconds would increment seconds and store 0 for milliseconds.
        Parameters:
        hours - integer input for number of hours
        minutes - integer input for number of minutes
        seconds - integer input for number of seconds
        milliseconds - integer input for number of milliseconds

    • Method Detail

      • getHours

        public int getHours()
        Accessor for the property hours
        Returns:
        an integer representing the number of hours in the elapsed time

      • getMinutes

        public int getMinutes()
        Accessor for the property minutes
        Returns:
        an integer representing the number of minutes in the elapsed time

      • getSeconds

        public int getSeconds()
        Accessor for the property seconds
        Returns:
        an integer representing the number of minutes in the elapsed time

      • getMilliseconds

        public int getMilliseconds()
        Accessor for the property milliseconds
        Returns:
        an integer representing the number of milliseconds in the elapsed time.

      • getTimeInWords

        public java.lang.String getTimeInWords()
        Constructs a string of words representing the elapsed time. Preconditions: all properties have values and it is assumed that milliseconds is the last, but not only non zero property.
        Returns:
        the time as a partial sentence.

      • toString

        public java.lang.String toString()
        adds leading zeros where needed to print the time in the form: nn:nn:nn:nnn including leading zeros
        Overrides:
        toString in class java.lang.Object
        Returns:
        a string representing elapsed time

      • compareTo

        public int compareTo(ElapsedTime that)
        compareTo (abstract method of the comparable Interface) is implemented to impose a natural ordering on a group of objects. compareTo is used by the Collections.sort routine to allow us to sort the times included in objects belonging to some Java collection.
        Specified by:
        compareTo in interface java.lang.Comparable<ElapsedTime>
        Parameters:
        that - the time we are comparing this time too
        Returns:
        a negative integer, zero, or a positive integer as this object is less than, equal to, or greater than the specified object.

      • main

        public static void main(java.lang.String[] args)
